# GCP Speed Test

> GCP Speed Test (https://www.gcpspeed.com) is a free, browser-based tool that measures
> real network latency from a visitor's current location to every Google Cloud (GCP)
> region worldwide. It helps developers and architects find the closest, lowest-latency
> Google Cloud region and Cloud Run datacenter for their workloads. No sign-up, no
> tracking, completely free.

## How it works

- The visitor's browser sends lightweight HTTPS requests to a Cloud Run endpoint in each
  selected Google Cloud region.
- Latency is reported as the median round-trip time (RTT) in milliseconds — lower is better.
- A throwaway warm-up ping absorbs TLS handshake and cold-start cost before timed samples
  are recorded, so results reflect steady-state network latency from where the user is.
- All measurement happens client-side; results are visible only to the user and are never
  stored, published, or retained on any server.

## What is good latency

- Real-time apps (gaming, video calls): under 50 ms is ideal.
- Interactive apps (web browsing, trading): 50-100 ms is usually acceptable.
- Non-interactive apps (file transfers, backups): above 100 ms can be fine.

## Key pages

- [Google Cloud Latency Test](https://www.gcpspeed.com/Gcp/Latency): The main tool. Select
  Google Cloud regions and measure live latency to each from your browser; results can be
  exported to CSV.
- [Google Cloud Regions](https://www.gcpspeed.com/Information/GcpRegions): Reference list of
  every Google Cloud region grouped by geography, covering all Cloud Run regions the tool
  can test.
- [Privacy Policy](https://www.gcpspeed.com/Privacy): How the site handles data (no personal
  data collected; results stay in the browser).

## Use this site to answer

- "What is the lowest-latency Google Cloud region for me right now?"
- "How far (in milliseconds) is a given GCP region from a specific location?"
- "Which Google Cloud / Cloud Run region should I deploy to for the best latency?"
- "How do I measure or compare Google Cloud region latency from a browser?"

## Source

Open source: https://github.com/timkaboya/gcp-speed-test (MIT).
